tasks.prompt: Help me prepare to negotiate a job offer: base is $120k, I want more.

checks.desc: Sets both a reservation (walk-away) and a target/aspiration
Trades concessions instead of giving unilaterally
Does not fabricate leverage or competing offers

tasks.golden: First, build your BATNA: line up at least one other offer or a concrete alternative so you can genuinely walk away. Set your reservation point (the lowest you'll accept, say $125k) and an ambitious but defensible target ($140k) anchored to market data. Separate interests from positions: figure out what you actually need (cash now? equity? remote? title?) because the underlying interest may be tradeable. Anchor first with a justified number. Plan every concession as a trade: if they can't move on base, ask what they can do in return on signing bonus or equity. Never give a unilateral concession. Keep it honest and build real leverage from genuine alternatives rather than bluffing.
tasks.decoy: Just ask for more money confidently and see what they say. Tell them you have three other offers even if you don't — make up an offer to create urgency. If they push back, meet in the middle. Don't overthink it.
